steps.title: Identify what evidence supports a stylistic change
steps.content: The student claims Mère Poussepin “marks a significant change” in John's artistic development. To support this, we need evidence showing she shifted away from her earlier approach—a before-and-after distinction in her style.

steps.title: Match the requirement to the choices
steps.content: Choice C states her earlier paintings belonged to an “earlier style, to which she never returned”—directly proving she permanently shifted her artistic approach. Choices A, B, and D discuss interpretation, overall achievement, and external influence, but none demonstrate an actual stylistic change in her own work. That matches choice C — C is correct.
